matching_rule: One base case matches when a promoter or gene-body variant is visible in the current sample. Three additional case families match when the whitelist mean beta, the gene-name-related mean beta, or the all-numeric mean beta resolves to low, medium, or high methylation. Variant prediction rules also receive the actual sample REF -> ALT change; curated allele-change rules take precedence when the observed ALT allele or exact change has a known direction.
disclaimer: Predictive theses in this database are literature-guided research summaries derived from the bundled gene interpretation bundle. They are designed for exploratory synthesis in the UI and should not be treated as diagnostic or therapeutic claims.
concrete_variant_prediction: The variant observed in this sample suggests a folliculin tumor-suppressor thesis: if the matched event is a pathogenic FLCN frameshift such as c.1285dupC, the concrete clinical prediction is Birt-Hogg-Dube syndrome risk context, including renal tumors, lung cysts, spontaneous pneumothorax, and fibrofolliculoma biology. Common or unclassified signals should not be upgraded to that clinical prediction without variant-level pathogenic evidence.

variant_prediction_rules.prediction: Observed FLCN c.1285dupC suggests a Birt-Hogg-Dube syndrome thesis because this recurrent frameshift disrupts folliculin. The concrete risk context is renal tumor surveillance, lung cyst or pneumothorax susceptibility, and skin fibrofolliculoma biology.
variant_prediction_rules.sample_change_template: This sample matched FLCN c.1285dupC with the actual DNA change {change}. Anchor the FLCN prediction to that observed REF -> ALT result instead of treating the variant as only a theoretical marker. The current preview does not infer zygosity or allele dose, so dosage-specific conclusions require genotype fields.
variant_prediction_rules.basis: Frameshift loss of folliculin function with tumor-suppressor consequences.

evidence.label: PubMed 22146830: phenotype risk in FLCN carriers

methylation_sources.label: Whitelist mean beta
methylation_sources.description: Uses the curated whitelist probes stored in the interpretation database for the current gene.

methylation_sources.label: Gene-name-related mean beta
methylation_sources.description: Uses rows whose gene annotation explicitly names the current gene.

methylation_sources.label: All numeric-row mean beta
methylation_sources.description: Uses every numeric beta value that survived preprocessing for the current sample.

cases.label: Gene variant found
cases.prediction: The variant observed in this sample suggests a folliculin tumor-suppressor thesis: if the matched event is a pathogenic FLCN frameshift such as c.1285dupC, the concrete clinical prediction is Birt-Hogg-Dube syndrome risk context, including renal tumors, lung cysts, spontaneous pneumothorax, and fibrofolliculoma biology. Common or unclassified signals should not be upgraded to that clinical prediction without variant-level pathogenic evidence. An observed FLCN variant places this sample in the curated FLCN research context. The strongest clinical interpretation for FLCN is rare-disease oriented. Germline loss-of-function variants cause Birt-Hogg-Dube syndrome, so the local database emphasizes rare pathogenic and tumor-suppressor biology rather than common-SNP association signals. The clinically important FLCN variants are usually rare truncating, splice, or deletion events rather than common polymorphisms.
cases.rationale: This is the base thesis that activates as soon as any promoter or gene-body variant is visible in the current sample.
research_focus: FLCN is the canonical gene for Birt-Hogg-Dube syndrome with renal-tumor, pulmonary-cyst, and pneumothorax risk.
The gene is also studied in lysosome signaling, TFEB/TFE3 regulation, and AMPK-mTOR pathway cross-talk.
Birt-Hogg-Dube syndrome tumor-suppressor biology.

cases.label: Gene variant found + high whitelist mean beta
cases.prediction: When a FLCN variant is paired with high methylation in the whitelist mean beta, the sample best fits a combined regulatory-context thesis. This pattern leans toward stronger regulatory restraint or reduced local accessibility around the locus. The variant side of the thesis is: The variant observed in this sample suggests a folliculin tumor-suppressor thesis: if the matched event is a pathogenic FLCN frameshift such as c.1285dupC, the concrete clinical prediction is Birt-Hogg-Dube syndrome risk context, including renal tumors, lung cysts, spontaneous pneumothorax, and fibrofolliculoma biology. Common or unclassified signals should not be upgraded to that clinical prediction without variant-level pathogenic evidence. FLCN methylation is best read as tumor-suppressor regulatory context.
